taxonomy_root.name: Animal Kingdom
taxonomy_root.description: All animals — multicellular, heterotrophic eukaryotic organisms.

vertebrates.name: Vertebrates
vertebrates.description: Animals possessing a vertebral column and a bony or cartilaginous skull.

mammals.name: Mammals
mammals.description: Warm-blooded, hair-bearing vertebrates that nurse their young with milk.

carnivores.name: Carnivores
carnivores.description: Mammals whose diet consists primarily of animal flesh.

lion.name: Lion
lion.description: Large social felid of sub-Saharan Africa, apex predator of the savannah.

wolf.name: Gray Wolf
wolf.description: Social canid living in packs, wild ancestor of the domestic dog.

polar_bear.name: Polar Bear
polar_bear.description: Largest terrestrial carnivore, superbly adapted to Arctic sea-ice life.

primates.name: Primates
primates.description: Order of mammals including monkeys, apes and humans.

chimpanzee.name: Common Chimpanzee
chimpanzee.description: African great ape and one of the closest living relatives of humans.

gorilla.name: Gorilla
gorilla.description: Largest living primate, herbivorous resident of Central African forests.

cetaceans.name: Cetaceans
cetaceans.description: Fully aquatic marine mammals: whales, dolphins and porpoises.

blue_whale.name: Blue Whale
blue_whale.description: The largest animal ever known to have existed on Earth.

dolphin.name: Common Bottlenose Dolphin
dolphin.description: Highly intelligent, sociable cetacean renowned for acrobatic leaps.

birds.name: Birds
birds.description: Feathered vertebrates with a keratinous beak and forelimbs evolved as wings.

raptors.name: Raptors
raptors.description: Diurnal or nocturnal birds of prey equipped with powerful talons and a hooked beak.

golden_eagle.name: Golden Eagle
golden_eagle.description: Iconic diurnal raptor of mountainous regions across the Northern Hemisphere.

great_horned_owl.name: Great Horned Owl
great_horned_owl.description: Large nocturnal raptor with prominent ear tufts, widespread across the Americas.

passerines.name: Passerines
passerines.description: Most diverse order of birds, encompassing more than half of all avian species.

sparrow.name: House Sparrow
sparrow.description: Small, ubiquitous passerine closely associated with human settlements worldwide.

robin.name: European Robin
robin.description: Familiar European passerine instantly recognisable by its vivid orange-red breast.

reptiles.name: Reptiles
reptiles.description: Ectothermic vertebrates with scaly or plated skin: lizards, snakes, turtles, crocodilians.

crocodilians.name: Crocodilians
crocodilians.description: Large semi-aquatic reptiles among the oldest surviving vertebrate apex predators.

nile_crocodile.name: Nile Crocodile
nile_crocodile.description: Africa's largest reptile and a formidable predator of tropical rivers and lakes.

alligator.name: American Alligator
alligator.description: North American crocodilian, symbol of the wetlands of the southeastern United States.

squamata.name: Squamata
squamata.description: Order comprising all lizards and snakes — the largest reptile order by species count.

reticulated_python.name: Reticulated Python
reticulated_python.description: World's longest snake, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia.

komodo_dragon.name: Komodo Dragon
komodo_dragon.description: Largest living lizard, endemic to a handful of Indonesian islands.

invertebrates.name: Invertebrates
invertebrates.description: Animals without a vertebral column, representing over 95% of all known animal species.

arthropods.name: Arthropods
arthropods.description: Invertebrates with a jointed exoskeleton: insects, arachnids, crustaceans, myriapods.

insects.name: Insects
insects.description: Most species-rich class in the animal kingdom, characterised by six legs and often wings.

honeybee.name: Honeybee
honeybee.description: Essential social insect, cornerstone of pollination and honey production worldwide.

monarch_butterfly.name: Monarch Butterfly
monarch_butterfly.description: Celebrated for its remarkable annual migrations spanning thousands of kilometres.

arachnids.name: Arachnids
arachnids.description: Eight-legged arthropods: spiders, scorpions, mites and harvestmen.

wolf_spider.name: Wolf Spider
wolf_spider.description: Solitary, fast-moving hunting spider that does not build a capture web.

emperor_scorpion.name: Emperor Scorpion
emperor_scorpion.description: One of the world's largest scorpions, native to West African tropical forests.

mollusks.name: Mollusks
mollusks.description: Soft-bodied invertebrates, often shell-bearing: gastropods, bivalves, cephalopods.

giant_pacific_octopus.name: Giant Pacific Octopus
giant_pacific_octopus.description: Largest living cephalopod, celebrated for its remarkable intelligence.

garden_snail.name: Garden Snail
garden_snail.description: Common terrestrial gastropod, emblem of slowness and a culinary staple.
